channel.title: Health - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: All Your Hantavirus Questions, Answered by an Infectious Disease Expert - WIRED
item.description:
1. All Your Hantavirus Questions, Answered by an Infectious Disease Expert  WIRED

  3. Why the hantavirus outbreak is different from COVID-19  Al Jazeera
  4. Why hantavirus is not like COVID, according to infectious disease experts  CBS News
  5. The hantavirus outbreak has been well-handled – but there are still dangerous days ahead | Devi Sridhar  The Guardian

item.title: P.C.O.S. Has Been Renamed P.M.O.S. Doctors Hope It Will Improve Care for Millions. - The New York Times
item.description:

  3. Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome: New name to improve diagnosis and care of condition affecting 170 million women worldwide  Endocrine Society

item.title: Study finds engaging with the arts can slow biological aging - NPR
item.description:
1. Study finds engaging with the arts can slow biological aging  NPR

  3. Engaging with the arts linked to slower aging at the biological level  Medical Xpress
  4. Enjoying the arts ‘has same effect on ageing as a weekly workout’  The Times
  5. The Guardian view on public health and the arts: the all-singing, all-dancing science of ageing | Editorial  The Guardian

item.title: More Cancer Patients Are Taking Ivermectin. Mel Gibson and Joe Rogan Might Be Why. - The New York Times
item.description:
1. More Cancer Patients Are Taking Ivermectin. Mel Gibson and Joe Rogan Might Be Why.  The New York Times

  3. Ivermectin prescriptions spiked after Mel Gibson touted it for cancer on Joe Rogan’s podcast  Scientific American

  5. Celebrity Endorsement Spurs Surge in Off-Label Ivermectin Prescriptions as Cancer Treatment, UCLA-Led Study Finds  Onco'Zine

item.title: Norovirus-hit cruise docks at Port Canaveral after 115 fall ill on nightmare voyage - WPEC

  4. Hantavirus and norovirus outbreaks on cruises raise alarms for passengers. How to avoid illness  ABC7 Los Angeles

item.title: Common Sweetener May Harm Critical Brain Barrier, Risking Stroke - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Common Sweetener May Harm Critical Brain Barrier, Risking Stroke  ScienceAlert
  2. Is your “sugar-free” soda causing your brain to age? A study of 12,772 people links artificial sweeteners to cognitive decline that is up to 62% faster  OkDiario
  3. Liquid sugar enters Alzheimer's radar: Framingham study with over 4 thousand participants links sodas, industrialized teas, and sugary drinks to smaller brain volume, worse memory, and silent signs of brain aging.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  4. It’s not sugar, but it’s not magic either: the “sugar-free” sweetener found in keto bars and diet sodas could set off the brain’s defense mechanisms  OkDiario
  5. The zero-sugar soda that seemed like a smart choice enters science's radar: a Brazilian study links artificial sweeteners to faster memory decline and accelerated brain aging.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
